method_atom: A large-population limit theorem showing that a system of N identical, pairwise-interacting units prepared in an uncorrelated (product/factorized) state has its exact microscopic evolution well approximated, as N grows large, by a self-consistent nonlinear mean-field evolution of a single representative unit interacting with the averaged field of all others. The result supplies quantitative bounds on the rate of convergence of the true many-body dynamics to the mean-field description, controlling the size of the fluctuations/correlations that develop around the mean-field solution.
object_atom: A gas of N interacting bosons, whose exact evolution is governed by the linear N-body Schroedinger equation and whose macroscopic mean-field limit is the nonlinear Hartree equation; the tracked quantity is the quantum one-particle reduced density compared against the projection onto the Hartree solution.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the propagation-of-chaos / mean-field limit with explicit convergence-rate bounds on fluctuations around a self-consistent single-unit evolution; the bosons, Schroedinger, and Hartree equations are the quantum-physics-specific object.
